themes.text: Still current at:17 July 2026 Updated:28 May 2026 Latest update:We continue to advise exercise a high degree of caution overall. To enter Sri Lanka, you'll need a visa in the form of an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA). Apply online before you travel, as there are limited facilities to obtain an ETA on arrival. The ETA is free for Australian passport holders travelling for tourism for up to 30 days. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Check the Sri Lanka ETA website for more details (see 'Travel'). We advise: Exercise a high degree of caution in Sri Lanka due to the threat of public demonstrations and security risks. Download Sri Lanka Download Asia

themes.heading: Tourist police
themes.text: Call +94 11 242 1052 or +94 11 238 2209. Latest update We continue to advise exercise a high degree of caution overall. To enter Sri Lanka, you'll need a visa in the form of an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA). Apply online before you travel, as there are limited facilities to obtain an ETA on arrival. The ETA is free for Australian passport holders travelling for tourism for up to 30 days. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Check the Sri Lanka ETA website for more details (see 'Travel'). Advice Level summary We advise: Exercise a high degree of caution in Sri Lanka due to the threat of public demonstrations and security risks.

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: Flooding and landslides occur during the monsoon season, from December to March in the northeast and May to October in the southwest. Be prepared to change your travel plans. Don't attempt to cross or enter floodwaters due to the risk of disease or injury. There is an ongoing risk of terrorist attacks. Terrorists may target areas popular with foreigners and tourists, including in regional and coastal towns. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East could lead to an increased threat of attacks against Israeli interests in other regions. Public demonstrations can occur throughout Sri Lanka. Demonstrations can lead to disruptions to traffic and public transport. Avoid areas impacted by demonstrations, follow the advice of local authorities, and monitor media for updates. Public emergencies can be declared, and curfews imposed at short notice. Carry relevant travel and identification documents with you at all times. Allow additional time to clear security checks, especially at airports. Crime, including sexual assault, harassment and robbery, can occur. If you're travelling alone, arrange travel through a reputable company. Be alert to the potential risks around drink spiking and methanol poisoning through consuming alcoholic drinks. See our advice on partying safely while overseas. There are marked and unmarked minefields and unexploded weapons in the Northern Province and parts of the Eastern Province. Stay on the main roads. Pay close attention to landmine warning signs. Scams and fraud are common, including credit card fraud, overcharging, and fake goods. Be alert to fake goods, especially jewellery and gems. Check your bank statements often. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Medical services in Colombo are below Australian standards. Outside Colombo, they're extremely limited. If you're seriously ill or injured, you'll need medical evacuation. Ensure your travel insurance covers this. Import delays may impact access to some medicines. There are shortages of some medicines. Mental health services are limited and below Australian standards. Dengue fever is a risk, with increased cases during the monsoon season. Other insect-borne diseases include chikungunya, Japanese encephalitis and filariasis. Use mosquito and insect repellent. If you have a fever, seek medical help. Ask your doctor about health risks and vaccines before you go. Foodborne, waterborne and other infectious diseases include typhoid, hepatitis, and leptospirosis. Drink only boiled or bottled water. Rabies is present in dogs, cats, monkeys, bats and other mammals. Avoid contact with animals. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: Public Emergencies can be declared, and curfews imposed at short notice. Carry relevant travel and photo identification documents with you at all times. If you don't, officials may detain you. It's illegal to cover your face in a way that prevents identification. Follow the advice of local authorities. The legal drinking age is 21. It is illegal to drink alcohol or smoke in public. Be careful when taking photos. You must not photograph or video inside High-Security Zones (HSZs). These include military sites, some government buildings and official residences. HSZs aren't always marked. Respect the local culture. It's illegal to mistreat Buddhist images. This includes posing for photos with Buddha statues or wearing tattoos, clothing or jewellery associated with Buddhism. Get local advice before photographing places of worship. Sri Lanka has conservative dress and behaviour standards. Take care not to offend. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: Sri Lanka has introduced a National Fuel Authorisation System requiring vehicles to be registered and issued with a QR code to access fuel. If you travel with a tour operator, you're unlikely to be affected. If you plan to hire a private vehicle, ensure it's registered and has a valid QR code. Check with your tour operator or car hire company before you travel. For more information see the Sri Lankan Government's websites on the Fuel Purchase Registration System and Fuel Rationing Programme. For additional assistance, contact the Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority hotline on 1912 (from Sri Lanka). To enter Sri Lanka, you'll need a visa in the form of an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA). Apply online before you travel, as there are limited facilities to obtain an ETA on arrival at Colombo's Bandaranaike International Airport. The ETA is free for Australian passport holders travelling to Sri Lanka for tourism. The tourist ETA allows you to stay for up to 30 days. If you plan to stay longer than 30 days, you must apply for a visa extension and pay the relevant fees. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Check the Sri Lanka ETA website for details about visas, currency, customs and quarantine rules before you travel. Airlines may require proof of certain vaccinations to travel. Check requirements with individual airlines prior to travel. Curfews may be implemented and travel restrictions imposed at short notice. The local currency is the Sri Lankan Rupee (LKR). Exchange Australian dollars at the airport or commercial banks in major centres. Most major towns and cities have ATMs, but not all accept international cards. Hotels and major shops accept credit cards. If you're carrying more than USD10,000 or equivalent in cash in foreign currency, you may be required to declare this on arrival and departure. If you're carrying more than USD15,000 in foreign currency in total in the form of cash, bank drafts, cheques, and travel cards, you must also declare this on arrival and departure. You may bring into or take out of Sri Lanka up to LKR 20,000 in Sri Lankan Rupees. Check with the Sri Lanka Customs Passenger Services Directorate for more information. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. For consular help, contact the Australian High Commission in Colombo. To stay up to date with local information, follow the High Commission's social media accounts. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: There is an ongoing risk of terrorist attacks. Terrorists may target areas popular with foreigners and tourists, including in regional and coastal towns. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East could lead to an increased threat of attacks against Israeli interests in other regions. Terrorism is also threat worldwide. Terrorism can occur anywhere at any time. Be alert to possible threats. Take official warnings seriously. Terrorists may target areas popular with foreigners and/or visited by tourists. To reduce your risk of being involved in a terrorist attack: avoid crowded areas always be alert follow the advice of local authorities check media and other sources A terrorist attack on 21 April 2019 targeted prominent hotels and churches in the cities of Colombo, Negombo and Batticaloa. Over 250 people were killed, and more than 450 injured. When you're travelling: carry travel and identification documents for any checkpoints allow additional time to clear security formalities be prepared to undergo increased security checks at Bandaranaike International Airport If there’s a terrorist incident, leave the area if it’s safe to do so and follow the instructions of local authorities. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Public demonstrations can occur throughout Sri Lanka and may become violent. Tear gas and water cannons may be used. Demonstrations can lead to disruptions to traffic and public transport. Follow the advice of local authorities and check media for updates. Public Emergencies can be declared, and curfews imposed at short notice. Carry relevant travel and photo identification documents with you at all times. To stay safe during periods of unrest: avoid all protests and large public gatherings check the news for planned or possible unrest obey curfews and seek local advice on safety and security follow the instructions of security personnel If you're detained, ask to contact the Australian High Commission (see 'Local contacts'). Security forces are visible, particularly in the Northern and Eastern provinces. Military and police have wide-ranging powers and may: establish checkpoints or road closures without warning impose curfews detain people without charge for long periods search people, vehicles, homes or commercial premises Authorities may apply travel restrictions for foreigners without notice. More information: Protests and civil unrest

themes.heading: Landmines
themes.text: Marked and unmarked minefields and unexploded weapons remain in some areas. Most are in the Northern and Eastern Provinces. Most of the Eastern Province has been cleared but some isolated areas remain uncleared. There are marked and unmarked minefields and unexploded weapons in the Northern Province and parts of the Eastern Province. Stay on main roads. Pay close attention to landmine warning signs.

themes.heading: Crime
themes.text: Pickpocketing, bag snatching, and other petty crime are risks. Be careful of theft in crowds and these locations: markets sporting events public transport hotels and guesthouses Avoid unlit areas and places away from crowds, including city streets, village lanes and beaches. Crimes can include: verbal harassment physical advances drink spiking To protect yourself: keep your belongings close, especially in crowded areas see our advice on partying safely while overseas

themes.heading: Scams and fraud
themes.text: Scams and fraud are a problem, such as: credit card fraud, including skimming traders overcharging foreigners for goods or services traders selling travellers fake goods, especially jewellery and gems Ask your credit card provider how to prevent credit card fraud.

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping can happen anywhere, even in places that seem safe. The Australian Government’s longstanding policy is that it doesn’t make payments or concessions to kidnappers. More information, including advice on how to reduce your risk of kidnapping: Kidnapping

themes.heading: Tours and adventure activities
themes.text: Transport and tour operators don't always follow safety and maintenance standards. This includes adventure activities, such as water sports. If you plan to do an adventure activity: check if your travel insurance covers it check operators are trained ask about and insist on minimum safety requirements always use available safety gear, such as life jackets or seatbelts If you don't see proper safety gear or you're not sure about the company's safety, choose another provider or don't do the activity at all.

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: Sri Lanka experiences severe weather, including flooding and landslides.

themes.heading: Natural disasters
themes.text: Cyclone Ditwah in November 2025 caused significant damage across Sri Lanka, particularly in central mountain areas. Most of the affected areas have been cleared and tourist destinations are operating as usual. However, work to repair damaged infrastructure, including rail routes, bridges and areas affected by landslides, will continue for some time. Check conditions with local authorities or your travel provider before you travel by road or rail to all central mountain districts, including Kandy, Nuwara Eliya, Ella, surrounding regions, and the Mahaweli River Valley. Follow instructions of local authorities. Tsunamis can occur. There is minimal tsunami evacuation route signage, and many tsunami warning towers are not operational. If there's a tsunami: know the emergency plan at your accommodation follow instructions and advice from local authorities follow evacuation orders If a natural disaster occurs: secure your passport in a safe, waterproof location or always carry it on you (in a waterproof bag) follow the advice of local authorities check media and other sources keep in touch with friends and family consult with your tour operator about disruptions to your travel plans More information: Sri Lanka Department of Meteorology Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System Disaster Management Centre Sri Lanka Tourism Development Authority Sri Lanka Flood & Emergency Updates

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Health risks
themes.text: Ask your doctor about health risks and vaccines before you go. Make sure your vaccinations are up to date.

themes.heading: Insect-borne diseases
themes.text: Dengue fever is a risk. Outbreaks of dengue occur in all regions. The risk of insect-borne illnesses increases during the wetter months (December to March in the northeast and May to October in the southwest). If you have a fever, get medical help. Refer to the Ministry of Health's Epidemiology Unit for the latest information on dengue cases in Sri Lanka. Outbreaks of other insect-borne diseases often occur, including: chikungunya Japanese encephalitis filariasis or elephantiasis To protect yourself from disease: use mosquito and insect repellent wear long, loose, light-coloured clothing

themes.heading: Other health risks
themes.text: Foodborne, waterborne and other diseases sometimes occur, including: typhoid hepatitis leptospirosis rabies To protect yourself: drink boiled water or bottled water with sealed lids avoid ice cubes avoid uncooked and undercooked food, such as salad get medical advice if you have a fever or diarrhoea Rabies is found in animals in Sri Lanka. It's 100% fatal. Avoid direct contact with animals, including dogs, cats, monkeys, bats and other mammals. Seek urgent medical care if an animal bites or scratches you. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical care
themes.text: Medical services and care in Colombo are below Australian standards. Outside of Colombo, facilities are extremely limited, especially for mental health and emergency services. Mental health services are limited and below Australian standards. There’s no agreement between Australia and Sri Lanka for free healthcare. Private hospitals will ask you to pay a deposit or prove you have insurance coverage before treating you. Public general hospitals are free for Sri Lankan nationals. Tourists must pay for all medical care. This can be very expensive. Make sure your travel insurance covers you. The Australian Government won't pay for these costs. Facilities are limited for scuba diving injuries. The only working decompression chamber is at the Sri Lanka Navy Base in Trincomalee. If you become seriously ill or injured, you'll need to be evacuated to a place with better facilities. Medical evacuation can be very expensive.

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for carrying or using illegal drugs are severe and include the death penalty. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: Local customs
themes.text: Standards of dress and behaviour are conservative. Respect religious traditions and objects. Respect local restrictions and observances around religious holidays. Take care not to offend. Full moon (Poya) days are celebrated once a month. Authorities ban the sale and purchase of alcohol and fresh meat on these days. Get local advice before you take photos of places of worship and nearby areas. You may cause offence.

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visas
themes.text: To enter Sri Lanka, you'll need a visa in the form of an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA). You should do this before you travel, as there are limited facilities to obtain an ETA on arrival at Colombo's Bandaranaike International Airport. The ETA is free for Australian passport holders travelling to Sri Lanka for tourism. It allows you to stay for up to 30 days. If you plan to stay longer than 30 days, you must apply for a visa extension and pay the relevant fees. If you engage in activities that breach your visa conditions, you could be detained or deported. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. Check the Sri Lanka ETA website or contact the High Commission of Sri Lanka for details about visas, currency, customs and quarantine rules before you travel. Check when your visa expires. You could receive a large fine if you overstay.

themes.heading: Yellow fever and Cholera
themes.text: If you're travelling from a country with yellow fever or cholera you need to show evidence that you've received the relevant vaccines. You need a yellow fever vaccination certificate if you've travelled to, or transited an affected country in the 9 days before you travel to Sri Lanka. Babies under one year old are exempt. Border authorities and airlines may request evidence of vaccinations. All travellers should carry proof of vaccination status in English. Find out about returning to Australia after exposure to yellow fever. More information: Sri Lanka Ministry of Foreign Affairs Countries with a risk of yellow fever (PDF 152KB)

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with 'X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The local currency is the Sri Lankan Rupee (LKR). You can change Australian dollars for LKR at: the airport commercial banks in major centres Most major towns and cities have ATMs. Some ATMs don't accept international cards. You can withdraw cash from Visa and MasterCard cards at most major banks. Hotels and major shops will accept credit cards. Before you travel, ask your bank if your cards will work in Sri Lanka. Check your statements, as credit card fraud and card skimming occur. If you're carrying more than USD 10,000 or equivalent in cash in foreign currency, you may be required to declare this on arrival and departure. If you're carrying more than USD 15,000 in foreign currency in total in the form of cash, bank drafts, cheques, and travel cards, you must also declare this on arrival and departure. You may bring into or take out of Sri Lanka up to LKR 20,000 in Sri Lankan Rupees. Check with the Sri Lanka Customs Passenger Services Directorate for more information. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Driving permit
themes.text: To drive in Sri Lanka, you need either: a valid International Driving Permit (IDP) endorsed by the Automobile Association of Sri Lanka (AA) a temporary Sri Lankan driver’s licence from the Department of Motor Traffic To apply for a local licence, you must pay the fee and provide: your Australian driver’s licence (digital driver's licences may not be accepted) a copy of your passport and Sri Lankan visa passport photos If you drive without an IDP or Sri Lankan driver's licence, you may not be covered by your travel or vehicle insurance.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Demonstrations in Sri Lanka can lead to disruptions to traffic and public transport. Driving in Sri Lanka is hazardous. Road accident injuries and deaths are common. Driving standards are poor. Vehicles aren't well maintained. If you plan to drive in Sri Lanka: learn local traffic laws and practices drive carefully and legally don't drink and drive More information: Road safety

themes.heading: Taxis
themes.text: If you have a local SIM card, ridesharing apps are available in major cities. Vehicle standards vary.

themes.heading: Public transport
themes.text: Demonstrations in Sri Lanka can lead to disruptions to public transport. Safety standards on buses and trains may be lower than in Australia. There are a high number of road deaths and injuries, especially on intercity buses. There have also been fatal accidents on railways in recent years. Take care of your belongings because theft occurs. See Safety. More information: Transport and getting around safely

themes.heading: Sea travel
themes.text: Piracy occurs in the coastal areas of Sri Lanka. The International Maritime Bureau (IMB) issues weekly piracy reports. More information: Travelling by boat Going on a cruise

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Curfews and other security measures could affect domestic travel, including flights. Check Sri Lanka's air safety with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. For consular assistance, contact the Australian High Commission in Colombo.

themes.heading: Australian High Commission, Colombo
themes.text: 21, Srimath R. G. Senanayake Mawatha (formerly Gregory's Road) Colombo 7, Sri Lanka Phone: +94 11 246 3200 Fax: +94 11 268 6453 Email: consular.colombo@dfat.gov.au Website: srilanka.embassy.gov.au X: Australia in Sri Lanka Facebook: Australia in Sri Lanka Check the High Commission website for details about opening hours and any temporary closures.

themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 의료기관 연락처
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카에는 각 지역별로 현지인들이 주로 이용하는 공립병원들이 있으나, 시설·의료수준 등이 열악하여 외국인들은 사설 병원을 주로 이용합니다. ㅇ 콜롬보 지역의 주요 사설 종합병원은 아래와 같으며, 영어로 의사소통이 가능합니다. - Lanka Hopital : 011-553-0000 (응급환자는 1566) : o 578, Elvitigala Mawatha, Narahenpita, Colombo 5 - Nawaloka Hospital : 011-557-7111 (응급환자는 1514) : No 23, Deshananwa H. K. Dharmadasa Mawatha, Colombo 2 - Asiri Hospital : 011-452-3300 : No 181, Kirula Road, Narahenpita, Colombo 5

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: ㅇ 국명 : 스리랑카 민주사회주의 공화국 (Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka) ㅇ 수도 : 콜롬보(상업수도) 스리 자야와르데네푸라 코테(행정수도) ㅇ 위치 : 동경 79.40도~81.55도, 북위 5.55도~9.55도 사이의 인도 동남부 인도양 상에 위치 ㅇ 면적 : 65,610㎢ (한반도의 1/3) ㅇ 기후 : 열대 몬순기후 - 연평균 기온 27도(가장 더운 5월은 평균 26~31도, 가장 시원한 12월은 평균 22~29도) ※ 상기 기온은 평균 온도이며, 지역과 일시에 따라 더울 경우에는 35도를 넘어가는 경우도 있습니다. ㅇ 민족 : 싱할라 족(75%), 타밀족(15%), 무어족(9%)로 구성 ㅇ 언어 : 싱할라어, 타밀어, 영어 ㅇ 종교 : 불교(70%), 힌두교(12%), 이슬람교(9.7%), 기독교(7.4%) ㅇ 화폐단위 : 스리랑카 루피(Rs)

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고 현황
themes.text: □ 범죄 현황 등 치안상황 ㅇ 스리랑카의 교육 및 의료 서비스, 치안 상황 등은 인근 서남아 지역 국가에 비해 높은 수준이며, 지속적인 경제성장으로 전체적인 빈곤 지수는 다소 완화되었으나, 계층 간·지역 간 빈부격차는 아직 상당히 큰 편입니다. ㅇ 수도권 도심지역에서는 비교적 치안이 양호한 편이나 현지인들이 자주 이용하는 버스, 철도역, 상가 등에 야간 통행은 삼가 하시는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: ㅇ 툭툭(삼륜자동차) 운전기사가 외국 관광객에게 목적지 도착 이후에 터무니없는 가격을 요구하는 경우가 있으므로, 미터기가 달려있는지 사전에 확인하시거나 탑승 전에 차비를 흥정한 후 이용해야 합니다. 특히 야간 시간대에 툭툭 운전기사가 인적이 드문 장소로 이동하여 강도로 돌변하는 경우도 다수 발생하고 있으니 주의를 요합니다. ㅇ 여행객이 혼자 외출하는 경우, 사기, 절도, 강도의 표적이 되기 쉬우므로 여러 명이 함께 이동하는 것이 좋으며, 택시를 이용할 필요가 있을 경우에는 요금 미터기가 있는 택시를 이용하고, 툭툭 이용은 삼가는 것이 좋습니다. ㅇ 스리랑카는 불교국가로서 선교 비자 소지자 이외의 선교활동은 금지되어 있습니다. 사원을 방문할 때는 신발과 모자를 벗어야 하고, 허가 없이 사진을 찍어서는 안됩니다. 또한, 각종 행정기관, 군사시설, 관광호텔 및 금융기관이 밀집해 있는 Colombo 1 지역(콜롬보 중심부)은 사진촬영이 금지된 곳이 많으므로 촬영 시 유의해야 합니다. ㅇ 외국여행객 사건사고의 80%는 복장이 단정하지 않은 상태에서 불교 사원을 활보하거나 부처님 동상을 배경으로 사진을 찍거나 신발을 신고 입장하는 등 스리랑카 문화를 존중하지 않아 발생하는 것이라고 합니다.

themes.heading: 자연재해
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카는 전 세계에서 번개가 가장 많은 국가 중의 하나로 우기에 많은 번개가 발생하고, 매년 이로 인한 사망사고도 빈발하므로 유의가 필요합니다. 낙뢰는 몬순Monsoon 시작기에 해당하는 3~4월/10~11월 오후나 저녁에 발생 가능성이 높습니다. 일기예보를 주시하여 발생 위험 지역에 접근을 삼가셔야 합니다. ㅇ 강수량이 많은 4∼6월 중 및 10월 중순∼2월 중순 경에는 주요 관광지를 포함, 지역에 따라 홍수로 인한 산사태 이재민 발생 등 피해가 대규모로 발생하고 있습니다. ㅇ 2004년 인도네시아에서 발생한 지진의 여파로 대형 쓰나미가 발생하였습니다. 당시 높이 10m 해일이 스리랑카 전역 해변마을 대부분을 범람시켜 약 35,000 여명이 사망하고, 주택 60,000 채가 파손되었으며, 500,000 여명의 이재민이 발생하는 등 큰 피해를 입혔습니다.

themes.heading: 유의해야 할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카 동·북부 지역은 경찰력, 의료기설·통신·교통 등 제반 기반 시설이 많이 미흡한 상태로 여행자 사고 발생 시 대사관의 대응수단, 능력이 많이 제한적이기 때문에 여행 시 각별한 유의가 필요합니다. 특히 지방간선도로나 인적이 드문 곳으로의 여행은 가급적 삼가시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 콜롬보 시내가 아닌 지방 등을 여행할 경우에는 사전에 교통 편, 숙소 등을 확실하게 마련할 필요가 있으며, 반드시 여권을 소지하고 여러 명이 함께 이동하며, 여행사, 회사 Agency, 단체 또는 지인의 도움을 받는 것이 좋습니다.

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : X ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : X ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : X ※ 스리랑카 정부는 2026.5.25.부터 한국을 포함한 40개국 국민 대상 관광 목적 전자여행허가(ETA) 수수료 면제 제도를 시행함을 발표 - ETA 승인을 통해 30일간 체류(2회 입국) 가능하며, 30일을 초과하여 체류할 경우 스리랑카 이민국에 연장 신청 필요(유료) - 동 제도는 관광 목적 단기 체류자를 대상으로 하며, 취업, 유학, 거주 등 관광 목적이 아닌 체류에 대해서는 별도 비자 필요 입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 모든 외국인은 스리랑카 입국 전 반드시 ETA를 사전 신청해야 하며, ETA 미승인 시 항공기 탑승이 거부되거나 입국이 제한될 수 있음에 유의 ㅇ 관광 및 단기방문의 경우 인터넷(www.eta.gov.lk)을 통한 사전 발급 권장 ㅇ 단기 방문 비자로 입국 후 거주비자 변경 불가 ㅇ 장기 거주 예정자는 주한스리랑카 대사관에서 사전에 입국 승인 비자(Entry)를 받고 입국하여 주재국 이민국에서 거주비자로 변경 가능 출국시 유의사항 등 - 여권분실자의 경우, 반드시 주재국 경찰 분실확인서 발급 후 출국 권장 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 : http://www.slembassykorea.com/eng/ ㅇ 해당국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : https://overseas.mofa.go.kr/lk-ko/index.do

themes.heading: 일반 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카 사람들은 긍정의 대답인 경우 머리를 약간 좌우로 흔들면서 표시하고, 부정의 경우 고개를 흔들지 않거나 머리를 완전히 좌우로 분명히 세게 흔들면서 의사표현을 합니다. ㅇ 매월 음력 15일(보름)은 POYA Day로 모든 관광소가 휴무이며, 상점 등에서 육고기나 술을 판매하지 않고 있으니 참고하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 호텔 Check-out 시 시간이 많이 소요되므로 충분한 시간 여유(적어도 1시간)를 두고 미리 Check-out 하시는 것이 바람직합니다.

themes.heading: 종교 관련
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카인은 불교도가 대다수이지만, 이슬람, 힌두교, 천주교, 개신교 등의 다양한 종교 활동이 가능합니다. ㅇ 그러나 외국인의 선교활동에 매우 민감한 편으로 선교 비자 소지자 이외의 사람은 종교·선교 활동에 유의할 필요가 있습니다.

themes.heading: 팁 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 일부 식당과 호텔의 계산서에는 세금(15%) 이외에 10%의 봉사료가 포함되어 있지만 이와 별도로 봉사한 직원에게 소액을 지급할 수 있습니다.

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 국제운전면허증은 스리랑카의 Automobile Association의 인증을 따로 받은 경우에만 유효합니다. ㅇ 자동차 운전석 위치가 한국과 반대입니다(오른쪽 핸들). ㅇ 교통량이 많은 출, 퇴근 시간의 경우, 신호등은 황색 상태에서 교통경찰의 수신호에 따라야 하는 경우가 많습니다. - 운전석 및 보조석의 경우 안전벨트 착용이 필수이며, 미착용으로 교통경찰 단속에 걸릴 경우 벌금이 부과됩니다. - 도로상에서 신호위반시에나 혹은 사전 예고 없이도 검문이 잦으며, 검문 시 교통경찰이 운전면허증, 차량보험증, 차량등록증, 차량등록허가증(Renewal Licence : 매년 갱신) 등을 확인하는 사례가 많으니 꼭 소지하기 바랍니다. ㅇ 콜롬보 일부 지역을 제외하고는 도로가 넓지 않고, 바닥이 파인 곳이 많아 고르지 않으며, 교통법규 및 신호위반 다발, 일반버스 및 툭툭(삼륜자동차), 오토바이 운전자 등의 부주의 등으로 교통사고가 다수 발생하고 있습니다. ㅇ 도로 사정 등으로 아침 이른 시간이나 밤늦은 시간을 제외하고는 교통체증이 아주 심한 편이므로, (장소와 시간에 따라서는 10km 이동에 1시간 소요) 충분한 시간을 두고 출발하는 것이 좋습니다. 한국의 고속도로 이동시간 보다 3배 정도 이동시간이 더 소요됩니다.

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고, 음주운전, 렌트카 사기 등 - 대형교통사고는 많지 않으나, 가벼운 접촉사고는 잦은 편이기 때문에 운전 및 보행 시 유의하셔야 합니다. - 자동차 보험 없이 차량 운행이 불가능하며, 교통사고 발생 시 주로 자가 보험으로 처리합니다. - 음주운전 단속이 잦으며, 단속에 걸릴 경우 운전면허증을 반납해야 하며, 벌금도 추후에 징수됩니다.

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생 시 경찰에 신고 및 재외공관 긴급연락처로 연락하십시오. - 경찰 핫라인 : 119, 경찰대표전화 : 011-243-3333 - 교통사고 발생 시 보험사에도 연락하십시오. ※ 스리랑카 인과 교통사고 시 쌍방의 과실인 경우 각자의 보험으로 처리하며, 스리랑카 인의 일방적인 과실인 경우에도 각자의 보험으로 처리하는 것이 용이합니다(관할경찰서에 신고 후 법원 판결을 받기까지 상당한 시일이 소요되며, 보상받기가 쉽지 않음). 이와 반대로 우리국민의 과실인 경우 철저히 보상받으려고 하니 상대방과 잘 타협하여 적정선에서 합의하는 것이 바람직합니다. [대중교통] ㅇ 스리랑카의 버스는 시설이 낙후하여, 냉방시설이 되어 있지 않으며, 불결한 경우가 많습니다. ㅇ 철도의 경우 노선은 스리랑카 전역에 깔려있지만 역시 차량 및 철로 등 시설이 낙후한 경우가 많습니다. 하지만 여행 구간에 따라 버스 보다 빠르게 이동할 수 있기도 합니다. ㅇ 현재 스리랑카에서는 택시 서비스가 활성화되어 있지 않습니다. 길거리에서 택시를 그냥 승차하시는 경우는 없고 미리 전화를 걸어 예약을 하고 이용합니다. 택시는 요금 미터기가 설치되어 있으므로 미터 요금을 지불하시면 됩니다. 하지만 장거리의 경우(공항에서 콜롬보 등) 미리 가격을 정하는 것이 좋습니다. - Kangaroo Cabs 011-258-8588 - Budget Taxi 077-729-9299 ㅇ Uber나 PickMe와 같은 차량공유 서비스는 매우 활성화되어 있습니다. 미리 모바일에 어플을 설치해서 픽업 장소, 목적지, 요금 결재까지 온라인으로 지정할 수 있기 때문에 따로 가격 흥정을 하실 필요는 없습니다. ㅇ 콜롬보에서 지방을 방문할 시에는 주로 운전사를 포함하여 차량을 임대(운행거리 당 계산)하는 경우가 많습니다.

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 도로상에 자전거, 오토바이, 삼륜자동차, 일반자동차 등이 다양하게 운행되고 있으므로 운전 시 방어운전이 필요하며 보행자의 경우도 주의를 요할 필요가 있습니다.

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카 기후는 고온다습한 열대 몬순성 기후로 연중 기온이 16℃-33℃, 습도는 70%~80%이며, 사계절의 구분이 없고, 서리는 고산지대에서만 볼 수 있습니다. ㅇ 콜롬보의 월평균 기온은 24℃∼31℃, 연 강수량은 2,365mm(93inch)이며, 기온이 가장 낮은 12∼1월의 평균기온은 25.5℃, 기온이 가장 높은 4∼5월의 평균기온은 29℃로서 그 한서의 차이가 3.5℃에 불과하고, 연중 고른 기온 분포를 보이고 있습니다. 동북 해안은 비교적 건조하여 연 강수량이 600∼1,800mm, 남서부 지방은 2,000∼4,000mm 정도이나, 중부지방은 습윤지대로서 5,000mm 이상을 기록하고 있습니다. 누와라엘리야 등 고산지대에서는 4℃-7℃ 기온 분포를 보이며 코트, 털모자 등을 착용하기도 합니다. ㅇ 강수량은 몬순에 지배되며, 지역에 따라 강우량과 시기가 큰 차이를 보이고 있습니다. 남서 몬순은 5∼6월 중, 동북 몬순은 10월 중순∼1월 중순에 내습합니다. 그러나 최근에는 기후변화 영향으로 기상변화가 일정하지 않습니다. ㅇ 날씨정보 사이트 : http://www.meteo.gov.lk

themes.heading: 전염병
themes.text: ㅇ 스리랑카는 야생 동·식물, 삼림, 습지대 등이 잘 보존되어 있으나, 위생·보건시설 등이 부족하여 전염병 등이 유행하고 있으므로 위생·보건에 유의할 필요가 있습니다. ㅇ 신종인플루엔자 A(H1N1): 스리랑카에서는 지난 2009년 6월 16일 첫 번째 신종 인플루엔자 환자가 확인된 후 2009년 595명의 환자가 확인되었고, 41명이 사망한 것으로 보고되었습니다. 2010 이후 현재까지 감염자가 확인되고 있어 재 확산이 우려되고 있는 상황이니 주의하시기 바랍니다. ㅇ 뎅기열(Dengue Fever): 2000년 이후 스리랑카에 급속히 확산되고 있는 병으로 매년 40-60명 정도의 사망자가 발생하다가, 2009년에 33,4000명 감염, 328명이 사망한 것으로 보고되었으며, 이러한 증가 추세는 현재까지 계속되고 있습니다. 특히, 콜롬보, 캔디 등 주요 도시에서, 그리고 깨끗한 곳에서 감염자 수가 기타 지역에 비해 상대적으로 많고, 우기 이후(1~2월, 7~8월)에 고여 있는 물에 모기알이 서식하므로 주변 환경을 건조하게 하고, 낮 시간 동안 뎅구모기가 활동하므로 장소매 옷을 입는 등 예방이 요망됩니다. 2018년 한해 동안 5만 명 이상의 뎅기열 환자가 발생하였습니다. 뎅기열병은 예방약이 없어 발병 후 치료할 수 밖에 없기에 사전에 주의해야 합니다. ㅇ 이외에도 쥐열병(Rat Fever-매년 3~4천 건 발생), 치쿠구니아(Chikungunya -매년 약 4만여 건의 의심 환자 발생), 일본뇌염(Japanese Encephalitis-매년 약 150건 발생), 광견병(Rabies) 등 각종 전염병·질병이 다발하고 있어 주의가 필요합니다.

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: ㅇ 한국보다 3시간 30분 늦습니다. 즉 한국시간 정오 12시는 스리랑카 오전 8시 30분이며 서머타임은 적용하지 않습니다. ㅇ 전력은 220V이며 57Hz입니다. 전압차가 심하고 단전이 잦으므로 전기제품 보호를 위해서는 정압기(Stablizer)를 사용하는 것이 좋으며, 국내에서 사용하는 60Hz 용 국산 전자렌지, 세탁기 등 전압차에 민감한 가전제품은 손상될 우려가 많습니다. ㅇ 싱할라어, 타밀어가 공용어로 사용되며, 영어는 연결어로서 통용되고 있으나 교육수준이 낮거나 도시 외곽에 갈수록 영어로 의사소통에 어려움이 많습니다. ㅇ 시내 은행, 환전소, 호텔 등에서 환전할 경우 여권이 필요합니다. ㅇ 인천공항 등에서 로밍 할 경우 스리랑카에서 국내 휴대폰을 사용하는 것이 가능합니다.
